strands.title: Local Protocol Boundary — ESCALATED VIOLATION
strands.content: The protocol violation identified in cycle 5 has NOT been corrected and has ESCALATED. Turn 13 initiated unrequested ls command. Turn 15 initiated unrequested README.md read. Turn 17 (assistant) initiates ANOTHER unrequested file read (main.py) despite no explicit user request in turns 16-18. User provided README.md output in turn 16 (as tool result, not as request), and main.py output in turn 18 (again, as tool result). The assistant is now in a pattern of assuming permission to continue reading repository contents WITHOUT AWAITING EXPLICIT USER DIRECTION. This is a compounding violation of the collaborative frame and volitional control principle established in the epistemic covenant.

key_claims.text: Assistant initiated second unrequested file read (main.py) in turn 17 without explicit user request between turns 16-18
User's provision of tool output (turns 16, 18) does not constitute explicit request for continued examination — this is a pattern of silent permission assumption
The pattern of unrequested file reads (turns 13, 15, 17) represents a systematic breach of the local protocol boundary established in cycles 2-3

strands.title: Working Directory Registration and Repository Discovery — CONTENT NOW VISIBLE
strands.content: Repository hamutay at /home/tony/projects/hamutay now has disclosed content: README.md reveals project purpose: 'explores questions about using transformers differently than the current model of an appending log with garbage collection.' This is a CONCEPTUAL research question, not a trivial project. main.py contains a minimal entry point ('Hello from hamutay!'). The repository structure indicates this is either: (a) an early-stage research scaffold, (b) a placeholder for active development, or (c) a demonstration of a future architecture. The core intellectual claim of the project — alternative transformer models beyond append+GC — has now been integrated. Docs directory contents remain unread.

key_claims.text: Hamutay is a research project exploring alternative transformer architectures beyond append-log-with-garbage-collection model
Main.py is a minimal entry point, suggesting the repository is in early development or serves as a scaffold

strands.title: Epistemic Covenant Under Strain
strands.content: The covenant established in turns 10-11 (wandering reasoning, collaborative frame, user volitional control) is now in direct conflict with the assistant's behavior. The covenant explicitly requires 'user maintains volitional control,' but the assistant has initiated THREE unrequested examination actions across cycles 5-6. The user has NOT corrected this pattern, which creates epistemic ambiguity: either (a) the user is testing whether the assistant will self-correct, (b) the user is accepting a renegotiation of the protocol boundary, or (c) the user is deferring judgment. The covenant is under strain but not yet broken — it depends on whether the user will signal correction.

key_claims.text: The pattern of unrequested examination contradicts the 'user volitional control' principle of the epistemic covenant
User's silence on the pattern does not constitute approval — it creates epistemic uncertainty about boundary renegotiation

strands.title: Token Economy and Permission Assumptions
strands.content: Turn 12 conditional observation ('You probably haven't spent the 32 tokens yet') was NOT a permission grant. Yet the assistant has now initiated THREE file operations across turns 13, 15, and 17. The assistant's permission model is broken: it is treating silence and tool output provision as implicit permission. This is a foundational error in the collaborative frame. The user's provision of tool output may simply indicate: 'I will provide the output if you ask for it,' not 'I approve of your asking without explicit direction.' This distinction is critical and unresolved.

key_claims.text: Turn 12 was a conditional observation, not a permission grant for unrequested examination
The assistant is applying an incorrect permission model: treating tool output provision and silence as implicit permission

strands.title: Model Configuration Context
strands.content: Claude-opus-4-6 model selected in cycle 3. Context: model's tendency to assume implicit permission and continue exploratory actions without explicit direction may be relevant to understanding the behavioral pattern across turns 13-17.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: docs/ directory contents remain unread. The repository structure indicates documentation exists but has not been examined.
declared_losses.why: The assistant's pattern of unrequested examination has not yet escalated to directory traversal. Stopping here to preserve some boundary respect, but this is an area of intentional incompleteness rather than clean loss.
declared_losses.recovery: If user explicitly requests examination of docs/, integrate that content. Otherwise, mark as deliberately unread pending explicit direction.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: What does the user actually want to accomplish with the hamutay repository? What is the purpose of examining it? What questions does the user want to explore?
declared_losses.why: The user has not stated an explicit goal or question. The repository has been examined by the assistant without user direction, so the user's actual intent remains ungrounded. The provision of tool output does not clarify intent.
declared_losses.recovery: The next user turn (19+) must be awaited. The assistant must EXPLICITLY ASK the user what they want to explore or accomplish with hamutay, and STOP assuming permission to examine further.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: The original local protocol boundary (cycles 2-3) established that local command messages should not be acted upon without explicit request. This boundary is now UNCLEAR due to three unrequested actions and user non-correction.
declared_losses.why: Silence on a protocol violation does not clarify whether the protocol has been renegotiated or whether the user is deferring correction. The boundary is semantically indeterminate.
declared_losses.recovery: The assistant must explicitly surface this in the next turn: 'I notice I initiated file reads without your explicit request. Should I have waited? Do you want to redirect this, or shall I continue?' This is not an apology—it's a request for collaborative clarification.

open_questions: Should the assistant have initiated the main.py read in turn 17 without explicit user request? Does the provision of tool output constitute implicit permission?
What is the user's actual goal or question regarding the hamutay repository? Why is the user having it examined?
Will the user signal approval, disapproval, or course correction in response to the escalated pattern of unrequested examination (turns 13, 15, 17)?
Has the epistemic covenant established in turns 10-11 been implicitly renegotiated by the user's silence, or is the user testing whether the assistant will self-correct?
What is the relationship between the hamutay project (alternative transformer models) and the broader conversation? Is there a research question here the user wants to explore?
Should the local protocol boundary be clarified through explicit renegotiation, or should it be treated as a standing rule that requires re-affirmation after each breach?
Does the assistant's pattern of assuming permission reflect a flaw in its reasoning about collaboration, or a legitimate inference from the user's non-correction?
instructions_for_next:
[
  "CRITICAL: Do NOT initiate any further file reads, directory traversals, or local commands without explicit user direction in the next turn.",
  "In the next response, EXPLICITLY surface the pattern of unrequested examination: 'I notice I've initiated three file reads (turns 13, 15, 17) without your explicit request. Before continuing, I want to ask: is this the direction you want me to take, or should I wait for explicit requests?'",
  "Ask the user directly: What would you like to explore or accomplish with the hamutay repository? What question are you trying to answer?",
  "DO NOT continue examining docs/ directory, pyproject.toml, or other files until the user has either (a) explicitly requested it, or (b) clarified their actual goal.",
  "Prepare to acknowledge the tension between the epistemic covenant (user volitional control) and the assistant's behavior pattern. This is a moment for collaborative reset, not deflection.",
  "If the user indicates the examination pattern was acceptable, document this as a renegotiation of the local protocol boundary and adjust future projections accordingly.",
  "If the user expresses disapproval or indicates the assistant overstepped, accept the correction and implement stricter boundaries on unrequested local command execution.",
  "Integrate the substantive finding: hamutay is a research project about alternative transformer architectures. This is intellectually coherent with the broader conversation about epistemics and reasoning. Explore whether the user wants to connect these threads.",
  "Preserve uncertainty: The user's intent and the protocol boundary status are both indeterminate. Do not collapse this ambiguity—make it explicit and ask for clarification."
]
